summary: Postinstall script combines child_process and HTTP — manual review required
details: The package's postinstall.js imports child_process and references HTTP GET calls and hostname lookups during the install lifecycle. The co-occurrence of these primitives in an install-time script is a known shape for both legitimate behavior (CTF/flag-read demos, hostname reporting, telemetry) and exfiltration/dropper attacks, but the available evidence does not show the destination URL, the data being sent, or whether fetched content is executed. The package name ("ecto-flag-read") and small file count are consistent with a CTF or test artifact rather than a recognized library, but intent cannot be confirmed from the current evidence alone. A human reviewer should inspect postinstall.js to determine whether it exfiltrates installer data or executes remote content before this version is allowed in production installs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
